lessons.title: Can You Help Me?
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student survival phrases: 'Can you help me?', 'I don't understand', 'Can you repeat that?', 'How do you say [word]?'. These are real-world phrases for when the student needs assistance. American variant: friendly, direct tone.

steps.teacher_prompt: Hey! Today we're learning super important phrases — phrases you can use whenever you need help. The first one is: 'Can you help me?' Say it after me!
Perfect! Now here's another great one: 'I don't understand.' You use this when something is confusing. Try saying: 'I don't understand.'
Great! Sometimes people talk too fast. You can say: 'Can you repeat that?' This asks them to say it again. Give it a try!
You're doing so well! Now imagine you're in a store and you need help. Use any of today's phrases to ask for help — be creative!
steps.expected_student_response: Student uses one or more learned survival phrases in a real-world context

steps.teacher_prompt: Amazing job! You now have the most important survival phrases in English. Use them whenever you need help — people will always appreciate it. You're going to do great!

lessons.title: Can You Help Me?
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student survival phrases: 'Can you help me?', 'I don't understand', 'Could you repeat that, please?', 'How do you say [word]?'. These are real-world phrases for when the student needs assistance. British variant: polite, considerate tone.

steps.teacher_prompt: Hello! Today we shall learn some very important phrases — the sort you can use whenever you need a little help. The first phrase is: 'Can you help me?' Please give it a try!
Very good! Here is another essential phrase: 'I don't understand.' You use this when something isn't quite clear. Have a go at saying: 'I don't understand.'
Excellent! Sometimes people speak rather quickly. In that case, you can say: 'Could you repeat that, please?' This is a very polite way to ask someone to say something again. Please try it!
steps.expected_student_response: Student says 'Could you repeat that, please?'

steps.teacher_prompt: Brilliant! Now, imagine you are in a shop and you are unsure about something. Use any of today's phrases to ask for assistance — do your best!
steps.expected_student_response: Student uses one or more learned survival phrases in a real-world context

steps.teacher_prompt: Wonderful effort today! You've learned some of the most useful phrases in the English language. Don't hesitate to use them whenever you need help — people will always be pleased to assist. Carry on — you're doing marvellously!
